projects.title: Unit 8 Project — Statistics in Action
projects.desc: Capstone: display your collected data with box plots and other graphs.

projects.title: Box Plot Builder
projects.desc: Construct box plots from data and read the five-number summary (6.SP.4).

projects.title: Box Plot Detective
projects.desc: Interpret box plots to compare and analyze data sets.

title: Display Data: Box Plots
contentObjective: I can make and read a box plot to summarize a data set.
languageObjective: I can explain my box plot using the words box plot, median, quartile, and interquartile range.

turnAndTalk.question: The Wildcats' scores are 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 18, 22, 24, 28. To build a box plot you need a five-number summary. What five numbers will you need?
turnAndTalk.kernel: A box plot is built from the minimum, Q1, median, Q3, and maximum.

stems.en: A box plot needs the ___, Q1, median, Q3, and ___.
stems.es: Un diagrama de caja necesita el ___, Q1, mediana, Q3 y el ___.

stems.en: The median of these scores is ___.
stems.es: La mediana de estos puntajes es ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students name the five-number summary (min, Q1, median, Q3, max) and find the median (14) for the 11 ordered scores.
turnAndTalk.extend: Justify: explain how to find Q1 and Q3 for this data set and what they represent.
extendStems: Q1 is the median of the ___ half, which is ___.
Q3 is the median of the ___ half, which is ___.

turnAndTalk.question: As you draw the box from Q1 to Q3, what does the length of the box tell you about the players' scores?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The box spans the middle 50% of the data; its length is the interquartile range.

stems.en: The box covers the middle ___ of the scores.
stems.es: La caja cubre el ___ medio de los puntajes.

stems.en: A longer box means the middle scores are ___.
stems.es: Una caja más larga significa que los puntajes del medio están ___.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: A strong answer explains the box covers the middle 50% (Q1 to Q3), its length is the IQR, and a longer box means greater spread.
turnAndTalk.extend: Compare: how would the box plot change if the highest score were 50 instead of 28? Which part changes, the box or the whisker?
extendStems: The box would stay about the same since ___.

turnAndTalk.question: Team A: Min=40, Q1=52, Med=60, Q3=68, Max=75. Team B: Min=35, Q1=55, Med=61, Q3=65, Max=80. A fan says Team B is better because they scored 80 once. Which team scores high more consistently?
turnAndTalk.kernel: A single high value matters less than the median and the spread of the middle 50%.

stems.en: Team ___ scores high more consistently because their ___ is higher.
stems.es: El Equipo ___ anota alto más consistentemente porque su ___ es mayor.

stems.en: The 80-point game is just the ___, not the typical score.
stems.es: El juego de 80 puntos es solo el ___, no el puntaje típico.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compare medians and Q1/Q3 (Team B has a higher Q1 and median) and explain the single max of 80 doesn't make a team consistently better.
turnAndTalk.extend: Critique: the fan focuses only on the maximum. What two box-plot features give a fairer comparison? Defend your choice.
extendStems: A fairer comparison looks at the ___ and the ___.
The maximum alone is misleading because ___.

turnAndTalk.question: A box plot has Q1 = 20 and Q3 = 36. What is the IQR, and what does it represent?
turnAndTalk.kernel: The IQR is Q3 minus Q1 and measures the spread of the middle 50% of the data.

stems.en: The IQR is ___ because Q3 minus Q1 equals ___.
stems.es: El RIC es ___ porque Q3 menos Q1 es ___.

stems.en: It represents the spread of the middle ___ of the data.
stems.es: Representa la dispersión del ___ medio de los datos.

turnAndTalk.listenFor: Students compute IQR = 36 - 20 = 16 and explain it is the range of the middle 50% of the data.
turnAndTalk.extend: Generalize: why is the IQR a more reliable measure of spread than the full range for a box plot with an extreme value?
extendStems: The IQR is more reliable because it ignores ___.
The full range can be misleading when ___.

vocabulary.definition: A graph that shows how data is spread using five key numbers.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Una gráfica que muestra cómo se reparten los datos con cinco números clave.
vocabulary.visual: A box from Q1 to Q3 with a line at the median, and whiskers from min to max
vocabulary.cloze: A graph that shows data with a box and whiskers based on the five-number summary is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: The middle number that splits the data in half.
vocabulary.definitionEs: El número del medio que divide los datos en dos mitades.
vocabulary.cloze: The middle value of the data, shown as a line inside the box, is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: Numbers that split the data into four equal parts.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Números que dividen los datos en cuatro partes iguales.
vocabulary.visual: Data: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14 → Q1 = 4 (median of lower half), Q3 = 12 (median of upper half)
vocabulary.cloze: A value that divides the data into four equal parts is a ___.

vocabulary.definition: The spread of the middle half of the data (Q3 − Q1).
vocabulary.definitionEs: La extensión de la mitad central de los datos (Q3 − Q1).
vocabulary.visual: If Q1 = 12 and Q3 = 20, then IQR = 20 − 12 = 8 — the middle 50% spans 8 units
vocabulary.cloze: The distance between the first and third quartiles, showing the middle 50% of data, is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: How the data looks: where it sits and how spread out it is.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Cómo se ven los datos: dónde están y qué tan separados están.
vocabulary.visual: A box plot where the median is centered in the box shows symmetric distribution
vocabulary.cloze: The way data values are spread out is the ___.

vocabulary.definition: How spread out the numbers are.
vocabulary.definitionEs: Qué tan separados están los números.
vocabulary.visual: Small IQR = low variability in the middle 50%. Large IQR = high variability
vocabulary.cloze: How spread out the data values are is the ___.

launch.narrative: The basketball league wants to compare scoring across teams. Here are the points scored by each player on the Wildcats in their last game: 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 18, 22, 24, 28. The league needs a quick visual that shows the spread, the middle 50%, and any high or low scorers. Time to build a box plot!
launch.contextImage: Basketball scoreboard with individual player stat lines
noticePrompts: What are the lowest and highest scores?
What score is in the middle of the data?
Are the scores bunched together or spread out?
wonderPrompts: How can you show the spread of all this data in one picture?
What does the 'middle half' of the data look like?

visual.title: Wildcats — points scored (box plot)
visual.caption: The box shows the middle 50% (Q1–Q3). The line inside is the median. Whiskers reach the lowest and highest scores.

conceptIntro.heading: What is a box plot?
conceptIntro.intro: A box plot is a simple picture that shows how a set of data is spread out, using just five key numbers: the lowest value, the first quartile (Q1), the median (middle), the third quartile (Q3), and the highest value.
conceptIntro.keyIdea: The box always holds the middle 50% of the data, and the line inside the box is the median.

iDo.title: Watch me build one
lines: My data, in order: 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 18, 22, 24, 28. There are 11 numbers.
The middle number is the 6th one, 14, so the median = 14.
The lower half is 4, 6, 8, 10, 12. Its middle is 8, so Q1 = 8.
The upper half is 15, 18, 22, 24, 28. Its middle is 22, so Q3 = 22.
The lowest is 4 and the highest is 28. I draw a box from 8 to 22, a line at 14, and whiskers out to 4 and 28.

weDo.title: Let's try one together
lines: New, smaller data set: 2, 5, 5, 8, 11. First, what is the median (the middle value)?
The lower half is 2, 5 and the upper half is 8, 11. What is Q1? What is Q3?
What are the lowest and highest values? Now picture the box stretching from Q1 to Q3 with the median line inside.

youDo.title: Now it's your turn
lines: On the next screens you'll find the five-number summary for the Wildcats' scores yourself: the minimum, Q1, the median, Q3, and the maximum.
Then you'll place each value on the number line to build the box plot — just like I did.

explore.label: Find the five-number summary for the Wildcats' scoring data: 4, 6, 8, 10, 12, 14, 15, 18, 22, 24, 28. Place each value on the number line.

discourse.prompt: The IQR is Q3 − Q1 = 22 − 8 = 14. What does this tell you about the middle 50% of scorers?
discourse.sentenceFrame: The middle 50% of players scored between ___ and ___ points. The IQR of ___ means the middle half of the data is spread across ___ points. This tells me ___.

optional.stem: The Hawks' points per quarter are 2, 4, 5, 7, 8, 10, 12. Resting on the scoreboard, what are the minimum, median, and maximum?
optional.explanation: The data is already in order. The smallest value is 2 and the largest is 12. There are 7 values, so the median is the 4th value, which is 7.

optional.stem: The Comets scored 6, 10, 14, 18, 22, 26, 30, 34 across 8 games. After ordering, what are Q1 and Q3?
optional.explanation: With 8 values the median splits the data into a lower half (6, 10, 14, 18) and an upper half (22, 26, 30, 34). Q1 is the median of the lower half: (10 + 14) ÷ 2 = 12. Q3 is the median of the upper half: (26 + 30) ÷ 2 = 28.

optional.label: Match each team's ordered scores to its five-number summary (min, Q1, median, Q3, max).

optional.instructions: Each card describes a part of the stadium box plot. Sort each card under the matching box plot part.

items.text: Q1 — the median of the lower half
Q3 — the median of the upper half

optional.stem: A box plot for the Rockets shows Min = 8, Q1 = 15, Median = 22, Q3 = 30, Max = 38. The middle 50% of their scores falls between which two values?
optional.explanation: The box of a box plot stretches from Q1 to Q3, and that box always holds the middle 50% of the data. Here the box goes from Q1 = 15 to Q3 = 30.

optional.title: Level 2 Extension — The Skipped Ordering Mistake

workedExample.label: Median
workedExample.work: The middle value of the list is 9, so Median = 9

workedExample.label: Q1 and Q3
workedExample.work: Lower half = 17, 5, 21 → Q1 = 5. Upper half = 13, 25 → Q3 = 25

optional.correctWork: The data must be ordered from least to greatest FIRST: 5, 9, 13, 17, 21, 25, 29. There are 7 values, so the median is the 4th value, which is 17 (not 9). The lower half is 5, 9, 13, so Q1 = 9, and the upper half is 21, 25, 29, so Q3 = 25. The student found the 'middle of the list' without ordering the data first, which gave the wrong median and quartiles.
hints: You can never find the median or quartiles until the data is in order from least to greatest.
After ordering (5, 9, 13, 17, 21, 25, 29), the true middle value is 17, not the number that happened to sit in the middle of the unsorted list.

approaching.stem: A box plot shows: Min = 10, Q1 = 15, Median = 20, Q3 = 28, Max = 35. What is the interquartile range (IQR)?
On a box plot, what does the line inside the box represent?
approaching.explanation: The line inside the box of a box plot always represents the median.

approaching.stem: What percentage of data falls between Q1 and Q3 on a box plot?
approaching.explanation: The box in a box plot contains the middle 50% of the data (from Q1 to Q3).

approaching.instructions: Put the steps for creating a box plot in the correct order.

categories.label: Steps (in order)
items: Step 1: Order the data from least to greatest
Step 3: Find Q1 (median of the lower half)
Step 4: Find Q3 (median of the upper half)
Step 5: Draw the box from Q1 to Q3 with a line at the median, and whiskers to min and max

hints: Always order the data first before finding any measures.
Q1 is the median of the lower half. Q3 is the median of the upper half.
approaching.label: Order the steps for making a box plot.

pairs.match: Smallest value in the data set
Middle value of the entire data set
Largest value in the data set

onLevel.label: Match each box plot term to its meaning.
Data: 3, 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15. Place the five-number summary on the number line.
hints: Median = 9 (4th of 7 values). Lower half: 3, 5, 7 → Q1 = 5. Upper half: 11, 13, 15 → Q3 = 13.

onLevel.stem: Two box plots show: Team A has IQR = 8, Team B has IQR = 22. What does this tell you?
choices: Team A's middle 50% is more consistent; Team B's is more spread out
Team B is a better team
Team A has a higher median
The IQRs don't tell you about consistency
onLevel.explanation: A smaller IQR means the middle 50% of data is more tightly clustered. Team A's scoring is more consistent in the middle range.

extending.title: Find the Box Plot Error
extending.correctWork: Q1 is the median of the lower half (3, 7, 9), which is 7. Q3 is the median of the upper half (15, 18, 21), which is 18. The student used the wrong value for Q1 — they picked 9 (the last value in the lower half) instead of the median of that half.
hints: Q1 is not just a value in the lower half — it is the MEDIAN of the lower half.
Lower half: 3, 7, 9. The median of these three values is 7, not 9.

extending.prompt: Two basketball teams played 9 games each. Team A: 40, 42, 44, 46, 48, 50, 52, 54, 56. Team B: 30, 35, 40, 45, 48, 50, 55, 60, 65. Find the five-number summary and IQR for each team. Which team is more consistent? Which has a wider spread in the middle 50%?
extending.sentenceFrame: Team A: Min=___, Q1=___, Median=___, Q3=___, Max=___, IQR=___. Team B: Min=___, Q1=___, Median=___, Q3=___, Max=___, IQR=___. Team ___ is more consistent because its IQR is ___.

extending.label: Match each box plot feature with what it tells you about the data.

pairs.term: Long right whisker, short left whisker
pairs.match: Skewed right — some high values stretch far from the box

pairs.term: Median line centered in the box

pairs.match: Middle 50% is widely spread — high variability

optionalActivity.name: Box Plot Stadium Bridge
optionalActivity.intro: Step onto the court and turn each team's scores into a five-number summary to power the stadium's box plot scoreboard!

connect.scenario: Two basketball teams' scoring data are displayed as box plots. Team A: Min=40, Q1=52, Median=60, Q3=68, Max=75. Team B: Min=35, Q1=55, Median=61, Q3=65, Max=80. A fan says Team B is better because they scored 80 points once.
connect.promptQuestion: Which team is more consistently scoring high? Use the box plot values to support your answer.
connect.prompt: Team A's IQR is ___ and Team B's IQR is ___. Team A's middle 50% scores between ___ and ___. The fan is wrong because ___. The box plot shows that ___ is more consistent because ___.

exitTicket.stem: A box plot has Q1 = 20 and Q3 = 36. What is the IQR and what does it represent?
choices: IQR = 16; it is the spread of the middle 50% of the data
IQR = 56; it is the total of Q1 and Q3
IQR = 28; it is the median between Q1 and Q3
IQR = 36; it is the value of Q3
exitTicket.explanation: IQR = Q3 − Q1 = 36 − 20 = 16. The IQR tells you the range of the middle 50% of the data.
